title: Electromotive Force and Potential Difference — Hard

questions.question: A battery provides $144\text{ J}$ of energy to drive charge through a bulb. If the current is $0.40\text{ A}$ and the bulb is on for $2.0\text{ minutes}$, what is the potential difference across the bulb?
questions.explanation:
First, calculate the charge $Q = I \times t = 0.40\text{ A} \times 120\text{ s} = 48\text{ C}$. Then, $V = W / Q = 144\text{ J} / 48\text{ C} = 3.0\text{ V}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A source with an e.m.f. of $6.0\text{ V}$ drives $2.0\text{ C}$ of charge through a circuit. If $10\text{ J}$ of energy is used by the components, how much energy was lost to internal resistance in the source?
questions.explanation:
Total energy provided by the source $W_{total} = E \times Q = 6.0\text{ V} \times 2.0\text{ C} = 12.0\text{ J}$. If components used $10\text{ J}$, the energy lost is $12.0 - 10.0 = 2.0\text{ J}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Despite its name, 'electromotive force' is not a mechanical force measured in Newtons. Why is the term 'force' considered a potential misconception in physics?
options: Because it represents energy per unit charge, not a push or pull
Because it only exists when current is not flowing
Because it is a vector quantity that points towards the positive terminal
Because it is only measured in static electricity
questions.explanation:
The source notes that e.m.f. is not a force in the mechanical sense; it is a measure of the work done or energy provided per unit charge to drive the flow around a circuit.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: An electric motor converts $900\text{ J}$ of electrical energy into $600\text{ J}$ of kinetic energy and some heat. If $3.6\text{ C}$ of charge passed through the motor, what is the potential difference across it?
questions.explanation:
The potential difference depends on the total electrical energy input per unit charge. $V = W_{total} / Q = 900\text{ J} / 3.6\text{ C} = 250\text{ V}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A cell drives a current around a circuit containing a lamp. Which statement is correct regarding the work done?
options: e.m.f. is work done by source on unit charge; p.d. is work done by unit charge on lamp
e.m.f. is work done by source on unit charge; p.d. is total work done by all charges
e.m.f. is work done by ammeter; p.d. is work done by voltmeter
p.d. is work done by source; e.m.f. is work done by the lamp
questions.explanation:
e.m.f. is the work done by the source (energy provider) to move unit charge. p.d. is the work done by the charge as it passes through the energy user (lamp).

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
